questions.answer: p-block elements are the elements present in the right-hand side of the periodic table, from group 13 to 18. They are also known as the elements of p-orbitals.
key_points: Right-hand side of the periodic table

questions.q: What is the general electronic configuration of group 15 elements?
questions.answer: The general electronic configuration of group 15 elements is ns² np³.

questions.q: State the general electronic configuration of group 16 elements.
questions.answer: The general electronic configuration of group 16 elements is ns² np⁴.

questions.q: What is the oxidation state of nitrogen in NO2?
questions.answer: The oxidation state of nitrogen in NO2 is +4.

questions.q: State the general electronic configuration of group 17 elements.
questions.answer: The general electronic configuration of group 17 elements is ns² np⁵.

questions.q: What is the general electronic configuration of group 18 elements?
questions.answer: The general electronic configuration of group 18 elements is ns² np⁶.

questions.q: What is the main difference between the electronic configuration of group 15 and group 16 elements?
questions.answer: The main difference between the electronic configuration of group 15 and group 16 elements lies in the number of electrons in the p-orbitals. Group 15 elements have 3 electrons in the p-orbitals, while group 16 elements have 4 electrons in the p-orbitals.

questions.q: Describe the trend in the atomic radii of group 15, group 16, and group 17 elements.
questions.answer: The atomic radii of group 15, group 16, and group 17 elements decrease as we move from left to right in the periodic table. This is because the effective nuclear charge increases as we move from left to right, resulting in a stronger attraction between the nucleus and the electrons, which causes the atomic radii to decrease.

questions.q: Compare the electronegativity of group 15 and group 16 elements.
questions.answer: Group 16 elements are more electronegative than group 15 elements. This is because the electronegativity increases as we move from left to right in the periodic table, and group 16 elements are located to the right of group 15 elements.

questions.q: What is the main difference between the chemical properties of group 15 and group 16 elements?
questions.answer: The main difference between the chemical properties of group 15 and group 16 elements lies in their ability to form molecules with other elements. Group 15 elements form molecules by sharing electrons, while group 16 elements form molecules by sharing electrons and also by gaining one or two electrons to form anions.

questions.q: Describe the trend in the ionization enthalpy of group 17 elements.
questions.answer: The ionization enthalpy of group 17 elements increases as we move from top to bottom in the group. This is because the atomic radii of group 17 elements increase as we move from top to bottom, resulting in a weaker attraction between the nucleus and the electrons, which causes the ionization enthalpy to increase.

questions.q: Compare the electronegativity of group 17 and group 18 elements.
questions.answer: Group 18 elements are less electronegative than group 17 elements. This is because the electronegativity decreases as we move from top to bottom in the group and group 18 elements are located at the bottom of the group.

questions.q: Describe the trend in the electronic configuration of group 18 elements.
questions.answer: The electronic configuration of group 18 elements is ns² np⁶. This configuration indicates that the elements have a full outer energy level, which makes them stable and unreactive.

questions.q: Compare the chemical properties of group 17 and group 18 elements.
questions.answer: Group 17 elements are highly reactive, while group 18 elements are unreactive. This is because group 17 elements have a half-filled or fully filled outer energy level, which makes them highly reactive. Group 18 elements, on the other hand, have a full outer energy level, which makes them stable and unreactive.

questions.q: Describe the trend in the atomic radii of group 17 and group 18 elements.
questions.answer: The atomic radii of group 17 and group 18 elements decrease as we move from top to bottom in the group. This is because the effective nuclear charge increases as we move from top to bottom, resulting in a stronger attraction between the nucleus and the electrons, which causes the atomic radii to decrease.

questions.q: Compare the ionization enthalpy of group 17 and group 18 elements.
questions.answer: Group 17 elements have a higher ionization enthalpy than group 18 elements. This is because group 17 elements have a half-filled or fully filled outer energy level, which makes it difficult to remove an electron, resulting in a higher ionization enthalpy. Group 18 elements, on the other hand, have a full outer energy level, which makes it easier to remove an electron, resulting in a lower ionization enthalpy.
